memberdef.argsstring: (const float *a, const float *b, const float *c, uint16_t *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_fma_f32_to_f16

briefdescription.para: FMA in FP32, store result as FP16: dst = a * b + c.

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint16_t *src, float *dst, int rows, int cols, int src_stride, int dst_stride)
memberdef.name: ck_fp16_to_fp32_2d

briefdescription.para: Convert 2D FP16 matrix to FP32 with strided access.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (actual data per row)

briefdescription.para: Convert FP16 row to FP32 (auto-select best implementation)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *src, uint16_t *dst, int rows, int cols, int src_stride, int dst_stride)
memberdef.name: ck_fp32_to_fp16_2d

briefdescription.para: Convert 2D FP32 matrix to FP16 with strided access.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (actual data per row)

briefdescription.para: Convert FP32 to FP16 in-place using scratch buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Useful when you want to downcast in place but need FP32 for computation. Writes FP16 to the lower half of scratch, then copies back.

parameterdescription.para: FP32 array to convert (will contain FP16 in lower bits)
Temporary buffer, must be >= n * sizeof(uint16_t)

simplesect.para: After this call, data should be treated as uint16_t*

briefdescription.para: Convert FP32 row to FP16 (auto-select best implementation)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *src, float scale, uint16_t *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_scale_f32_to_f16

briefdescription.para: Scale FP32 array and store as FP16: dst = scale * src.

sect1.title: CK-ENGINE KERNEL RULES:

listitem.para: NO malloc/free - memory via bump allocator, pointers passed in
NO OpenMP - parallelization at orchestrator/codegen layer
NO memcpy for layout - use strided access, not copies
API must define: inputs, outputs, workspace, and memory layouts
Pure computation - deterministic, no side effects

sect1.para: After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

These conversion functions use F16C hardware instructions (available on Intel Ivy Bridge and later, AMD Piledriver and later) for fast FP16/FP32 conversion. FP16 (IEEE 754 half-precision) provides 2x memory savings with ~0.1% precision loss for KV cache storage.

sect1.title: MEGA-FUSION BENEFIT:
sect1.para:
FP16 KV cache doubles the context that fits in L3 cache:
FP32 KV: ~6K tokens in 6MB L3
FP16 KV: ~12K tokens in 6MB L3 This extends mega-fusion's "hot zone" for longer sequences.
